behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the expected interface from the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real model output quality. The failure is the model producing wrong parameter names/types/return type from NL instructions. A stubbed model can be forced to pass and would hide this failure (MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow because this behavior is about one generation result, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with interface-constrained prompts (typed params + return type), parse the generated signature, and assert exact match to expected interface over repeated runs to expose nondeterministic signature drift.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided tests on first or later attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: real LLM code quality (LLM-DEP). The behavior’s correctness depends on whether generated code actually solves the task; deterministic stubs do not expose this. Rejected Workflow: behavior text allows first-attempt success, so multiple real agentic steps are not strictly required by this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM generation for a representative task and run the produced code against the real provided test suite; assert service reports success when tests pass (whether on attempt 1 or a later attempt).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level Workflow was considered but disqualified by behavior text: "on the first or a subsequent attempt" does not require a multi-step agentic chain in every valid case.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt includes prior failure messages.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: module handoff correctness between test runner output and prompt-construction/orchestration logic (MOCK-MISS at module boundary). No real OS/network/subprocess is required for this failure mode, and correctness does not depend on model judgment quality. Therefore Integration is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + prompt builder + failing test-result producer in-process; force an initial failed attempt and assert the next LLM request payload contains the exact failure messages from the prior test run.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected because behavior is prompt-content wiring, not real infra behavior. Agentic rejected because behavior does not require evaluating model output quality.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error including all attempts and test results.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: retry-loop state management and error aggregation across components. This is cross-module orchestration, not pure single-function logic. Real OS/network/subprocess is not inherently required to catch this specific failure; deterministic failing attempt fixtures are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with deterministic failing generation/test outcomes for three attempts; assert no 4th attempt occurs and returned error payload contains exactly three attempt records with their corresponding test results.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System was considered and rejected because the behavior text is about stop-condition and structured aggregation, which can fail entirely in-process.

behaviors.description: End-to-end loop (generate → test → refine → retest) succeeds on a task usually missed by single-shot generation.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: multiple real agentic steps in sequence (MULTI-STEP) plus model-output quality improvement from feedback (LLM-DEP). A stubbed or single-step setup would miss real iterative-learning failures. This explicitly matches Workflow by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known hard prompt where one-shot real LLM commonly fails, run the full real loop with at least one refine cycle, and assert final implementation passes the real test suite while recording intermediate failed attempt(s).
